---
title: "Final Project"
subtitle: "STAT 40233 - Bayesian Methods"
author:
  - Braedon
  - Jayden
  - Mallory
  - Matthew
format: pdf
---

```{r, eval=F}
library(rstanarm)
library(dplyr)
library(bayesplot)
library(coda)

load("concrete_data.RData")
attach(concrete_train)

# Initial data check
plot(cement, ccs)
plot(slag, ccs)
plot(ash, ccs)
plot(water, ccs)
plot(superplasticizer, ccs)
plot(coarse_agg, ccs)
plot(fine_agg, ccs)
plot(age, ccs)

# Apply boxcox and add column for cement/water ratio
boxcox <- 0.75
new_data <- concrete_train %>% 
  mutate(ccs = ccs ^ boxcox, cement_water = cement/water)

# Check boxcox transformation
hist(ccs, main="Distribution of CCS")
hist(new_data$ccs, main="Distribution of CCS after Box-Cox Tranformation")

# Create 80% training and test data split
n <- nrow(new_data)
n_train <- n * 0.8
n_test <- n - n_train
train_index <- sample(1:n, n_train, replace = F)
con_train <- new_data[train_index,]
con_test <- new_data[-train_index,]

# Linear Regression Model
concrete_sim <- stan_glm(ccs ~ cement_water + ash + slag + age,
                         data = con_train,
                         family = gaussian,
                         chains = 2, iter = 6000, warmup = 1000,
                         seed = 303, 
                         prior = normal(0, 0.5))

summary(concrete_sim)
concrete_sim$coefficients
posterior_interval(concrete_sim, prob=0.95) # Check predictor significance

# Check autocorrelation
concrete_arr <- as.array(concrete_sim)
mcmc_acf(concrete_arr, pars = c("(Intercept)","cement_water", "ash"))
mcmc_acf(concrete_arr, pars = c("slag", "age", "sigma"))

# Test PPD on test data
concrete_post <- posterior_predict(concrete_sim, newdata = con_test)
concrete_means <- apply(concrete_post, 2, mean)
hist(concrete_means, xlim = c(0,30), breaks=20) # predictions
hist(con_test$ccs, xlim = c(0,30), breaks=20) # observations

# Calculate RMSE on test data
sqrt(mean((concrete_sim$fitted.values-con_test$ccs)^2))

# Run model again on full data
concrete_sim <- stan_glm(ccs ~ cement_water + ash + slag + age, 
                         data = new_data,
                         family = gaussian,
                         chains = 2, iter = 6000, warmup = 1000,
                         seed = 303, 
                         prior = normal(0, 0.5))

summary(concrete_sim) # get n_eff
concrete_sim$coefficients

# Test convergence with Geweke
con_mcmc <- rstan::As.mcmc.list(concrete_sim$stanfit)
geweke.diag(con_mcmc, frac1=0.1, frac2=0.5)

# Gelman
rhat(concrete_sim)


# Submission Data
concrete_post <- posterior_predict(concrete_sim)
prediction_means <- colMeans(concrete_post)^(1/boxcox)  # Reverse boxcox
prediction_intervals <- posterior_interval(concrete_post, prob = 0.95)^(1/boxcox)

```

Chosen Model Formula: Cement/Water ratio + Boxcox, no thinning, then remove insignificant predictors

Default priors, rmse = 6.864544

(Intercept)  -1.42727358 0.46924066
cement_water  5.74355019 6.55091549
ash           0.02401431 0.03250394
slag          0.02400805 0.03010860
age           0.03098522 0.03837655
sigma         3.12348397 3.43969950


Prior = normal(0, 1), rmse = 6.770818

(Intercept)  -0.91190703 0.98479014
cement_water  5.51328671 6.30318305
ash           0.02281480 0.03143361
slag          0.02320013 0.02944707
age           0.03074514 0.03825042
sigma         3.12723910 3.44685123


Prior = normal(0, 10), rmse = 6.863457

(Intercept) -1.40309904 0.46893694
cement_water  5.75399746 6.54177679
ash           0.02392273 0.03241529
slag          0.02404288 0.03017307
age           0.03098629 0.03846469
sigma         3.12580573 3.44449065


Prior = normal(0, 0.5), rmse = 6.548008 <- Best RMSE

(Intercept)  1.03013077 3.03753983  <- Now it's significant!
cement_water 4.53321608 5.35057759
ash          0.01705192 0.02665150
slag         0.01935043 0.02630400
age          0.03141275 0.04041018
sigma        3.13347090 3.50895293


After running best model on full data:

Coefficients:
(Intercept) cement_water          ash         slag          age 
 1.38547921   5.26805014   0.02419109   0.02433200   0.03374016 

              n_eff
(Intercept)   5324
cement_water  5975
ash           8185
slag          9509
age           13364
sigma         6922
mean_PPD      8759
log-posterior 4883

Geweke Test - Converged
Gelman Test - Almost perfect convergence (each was barely below 1)
Most significant predictor = cement/water ratio, has largest beta
